items.title: Tech Stocks Tumble On AI Nightmare, Nvidia Eyes Worst Day Since March 2020, Bitcoin Drops Below $100,000: What's Driving Markets Monday?
items.author: Piero Cingari
items.description: Tech stocks plummet as China's DeepSeek unveils an AI model outperforming ChatGPT, triggering fears of rising competition. Nvidia loses $450B, leading the Magnificent Seven's $600B market wipeout, marking its worst session since March 2020. Bitcoin drops below $99,000 as risk-off sentiment takes hold.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article mentions a broad selloff in tech stocks, which would likely impact Apple as a major tech company.
The article states that Broadcom crashed 17.5% due to the tech selloff.
The article notes that crypto-related stocks like Coinbase fell due to the risk-off sentiment, with Coinbase dropping 9.6%.
The article highlights that Nvidia lost $450 billion, or 15% of its valuation, marking its worst single-day loss since March 2020.
The article states that Marvell Technology crashed 17.5% due to the tech selloff.
The article mentions that Micron Technology sank 11% as part of the broader tech selloff.
The article notes that crypto-related stocks like MicroStrategy fell due to the risk-off sentiment, with MicroStrategy dropping 7.8%.
The article states that SoFi Technologies plummeted 12% due to a downbeat guidance, despite better-than-expected quarterly results.
The article states that the iShares Semiconductor ETF plunged over 7%, eyeing its worst session since the pandemic-driven selloff of March 2020.
The article notes that the Nasdaq 100, represented by the Invesco QQQ Trust, tumbled 3%, eyeing its steepest drop since December 2018.
The article mentions that the S&P 500, represented by the SPDR S&P 500 ETF, fell 1.8% as part of the broader market selloff.
